summary_one_line: A well-formed DOCTYPE/DTD content model with an OPT-quantified element so the automaton epsilon transition gets a non-NULL target state.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must be a syntactically valid XML document with a DOCTYPE declaration and a DTD element content model containing an optional ('?') quantifier (XML_ELEMENT_CONTENT_OPT), so xmlAutomataNewEpsilon is called with a non-NULL 'to' state and the false branch of 'if (to == NULL)' at line 6340 is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner (value_profile_cmplog) resolves 8/10 and hits the branch with avg hitcount 3; its per-role coverage takes the OPT case (line 671 case XML_ELEMENT_CONTENT_OPT marked [W]) and reaches line 6342 return(to), the false-branch sink marked [W]. CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback rewards partial-match progress through the many string comparisons in the DTD/validation path (xmlIsDocNameChar, xmlGetDtdElementDesc), building the structured prefix needed to reach the OPT-quantified content model.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser (cmplog) blocks 9/10 with avg hitcount 0 on the branch; its inputs drive the XML_ELEMENT_CONTENT_MULT case (line 682 marked [L]) which calls xmlAutomataNewEpsilon with NULL 'to', taking the true branch (line 6341 return(am->state) marked [L]). Lacking CMP_MAP gradient feedback, cmplog gets no incremental reward for partial matches and does not converge on the OPT content-model bytes.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming-distance / matching-prefix buckets) is the only technique value_profile_cmplog has that cmplog lacks; it preserves inputs that bring DTD-parsing comparisons closer to matching, guiding the fuzzer toward the optional-quantifier content model that supplies a non-NULL automaton target state.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same structural feature: a valid DOCTYPE/DTD whose element content model uses the optional ('?') quantifier so xmlAutomataNewEpsilon receives a non-NULL 'to' and takes the false branch at line 6340.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The winner (value_profile_cmplog) resolves 8/10 with avg hitcount 3 and reaches line 6342 return(to) [W] via the OPT case [W]. Its winning seed 4bed379e31cead31 carries the literal XML scaffolding bytes (<?xml version="1.0"?> and <!DOCTYPE) and its ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows, indicating I2SRandReplace spliced logged comparison operands (DTD keyword/element literals) into the input to satisfy the string comparisons gating the content-model path.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The loser (value_profile) blocks 9/10 with avg hitcount 1; lacking I2SRandReplace it cannot directly substitute logged CMP operands and so fails to reliably assemble the exact DTD literals needed to drive the OPT content model that yields a non-NULL automaton target.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace (present in value_profile_cmplog, absent in value_profile) reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the matching operand at the input offset, placing the exact DTD/element literals required to reach the optional-quantifier branch; this is the single technique delta between the pair.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of 'if (to == NULL)' in xmlAutomataNewEpsilon at xmlregexp.c:6340.
The winner takes line 6342 return(to) (false-branch sink) while the loser takes line 6341 return(am->state) (true branch).
The winner-resolving path enters the XML_ELEMENT_CONTENT_OPT case which calls xmlAutomataNewEpsilon with a non-NULL state.
The loser-blocking path enters the XML_ELEMENT_CONTENT_MULT case which calls xmlAutomataNewEpsilon with NULL.
At the blocker the winner takes the false side and the loser takes the true side.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L6340  T=0 F=1  T=3 F=0  if (to == NULL)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Pair 1 winner value_profile_cmplog beats cmplog with the technique delta being value_profile.
Pair 2 winner value_profile_cmplog beats value_profile with the technique delta being I2S.
value_profile's CMP_MAP rewards inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching than previously seen, preserving them as corpus.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: I2SRandReplace scans the input for byte sequences matching one CMP operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: The winning vpc seed carries XML/DOCTYPE scaffolding literals in its bytes.
value_profile_cmplog includes I2SRandReplace among its mutators.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 7311 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 4bed379e31cead31`; the ancestor chain has 8 levels with two I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 6 and depth 7, the lower-bound signal of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in vpc under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the 'to != NULL' epsilon transition behind an exact DTD literal where value_profile_cmplog resolves at the same rate as cmplog AND value_profile (no separation), showing neither I2S substitution nor CMP_MAP gradient is required to reach the optional-quantifier content model.

weakest_evidence_point: The OPT-vs-MULT case distinction is inferred from the per-role [W]/[L] tags on the caller switch, not from a direct trace proving the winner's specific input bytes drive XML_ELEMENT_CONTENT_OPT.
